fallback.reason: No standalone callable local cangjie skill was available in this runtime. The requested fallback path was used, with book2skill guidance and skill-creator structure applied.

metadataSupplemented.reason: The local source only listed Michael Pinedo's scheduling book entry and related books. Additional metadata/context was needed to make the skill self-contained and operational.
items: Identified the entry as Michael L. Pinedo's Scheduling: Theory, Algorithms, and Systems.
Recorded edition years from the local NYU source: 1994, 2001, 2008, 2012, 2016, and 2022.
Supplemented sixth-edition DOI metadata from Springer: 10.1007/978-3-031-05921-6.

Supplemented topic structure from the linked NYU book page and table-of-contents page, including deterministic preliminaries, framework and notation, schedule classes, complexity hierarchy, single-machine objectives, tardiness, earliness/tardiness, bicriteria problems, and trade-off curves.

closureReview.scope: Closed-loop review of SKILL.md, test-prompts.json, and audit.json.

qualityChecks.standaloneKnowledge: SKILL.md defines scheduling instance setup, objectives, eligibility, dispatching, fairness, preemption, verification, recovery, and boundaries without requiring source files.
qualityChecks.runtimeDependencyPolicy: External files, webpages, original books, source reports, external source folder, distilled source material, and out-of-directory material are provenance only and not runtime dependencies.
qualityChecks.triggerContract: Trigger, non-trigger, activation gate, and execution gate are explicit.
qualityChecks.workflowAndDeliverables: Workflow, scheduler contract deliverables, failure/recovery/idempotency, and hard rules are present.
qualityChecks.testPromptCoverage: test-prompts.json includes should_trigger, should_not_trigger, edge_cases, and compliance_expectations.

remainingRisks: Source snapshot focused on book metadata, but all runtime scheduling guidance needed by the skill is compressed into SKILL.md.

runtimeDependencyPolicy: Closed skill: execute from SKILL.md; use references/source-notes.md only as local optional provenance; do not require external source folders, webpages, original books, source reports, machine-local paths, or cross-skill files.
